stories.headline: Salesforce Launches Agentforce Help Agent with Pay-Per-Resolution Pricing, Goes GA July 2026
stories.summary: Salesforce has released Agentforce Help Agent as generally available this month, featuring guided setup deployable in minutes and a pay-per-resolution pricing model — customers are only charged when the agent fully resolves a customer issue. The product is built on the Agentforce 360 Platform and pre-grounded on Salesforce Knowledge, directly addressing the 'messy data' problem that most agent deployments fail on. For D&B, this is a live signal: Salesforce is making agentic AI dramatically easier to deploy at scale, which accelerates the urgency for D&B's entity-resolved, trusted data to be pre-embedded as the ground-truth layer inside Agentforce workflows before competitors fill that slot.

stories.headline: Zoom Acquires Common Room, Deepening the Sales Tech Consolidation Wave
stories.summary: Common Room, a platform that tracks community and product-led growth signals, is being acquired by Zoom — a notable follow-on to the Apollo/Pocus deal covered in prior briefings. The move signals that buying signal intelligence and PLG data are now table-stakes additions for any communications or GTM platform looking to compete in agentic selling. For D&B, this consolidation wave is a strategic opportunity: as signal-layer platforms get absorbed into large suites, the demand grows for a neutral, verified commercial data foundation (DUNS, Paydex, corporate family trees) that any consolidated platform can plug into, rather than relying on proprietary but shallow crowdsourced signals.

stories.headline: Agentic AI Pilot-to-Production Gap Widens: 70% of Enterprise Projects Hit Data Infrastructure Failures
stories.summary: A synthesis of Gartner, Deloitte, and McKinsey data published through July 2026 shows that while 100% of enterprises have agentic AI on their roadmap, 70% discover fundamental data infrastructure gaps only after launching initiatives — and most projects stall before reaching production scale. Deloitte's 2026 State of AI report pegs average enterprise ROI on scaled agentic AI at 171%, but notes that poor data foundations are the single most cited failure driver. This directly validates D&B's 'Explainability First / Ground Truth' positioning: the bottleneck is not model capability but data quality and entity resolution, which is precisely D&B's structural moat.

stories.headline: CFPB Releases 2026 Regulatory Agenda: Small Business Lending Data Rule Takes Effect July 2026
stories.summary: The CFPB published its 2026 regulatory agenda on July 6, confirming that a final rule reconsidering the Section 1071 small business lending data collection requirements takes effect this month, while a Financial Data Transparency Act joint rule with Treasury and the Fed establishing data standards for financial regulatory reporting is also anticipated in July 2026. The agenda reflects a deregulatory tilt under the current administration, with multiple prior rules being reconsidered. For D&B, the FDTA rule is the more important watch item: standardized financial data reporting requirements create regulatory pull for verified commercial entity data and structured business credit reporting — squarely in D&B's core competency.

stories.headline: Future B2B Launches 'Affinity' Real-Time Audience Data Platform Targeting Active B2B Buyers
stories.summary: Future B2B, a subsidiary of Future Plc, launched Affinity in June 2026 — a new audience data platform powered by first-party data from nearly 10 million business leaders, designed to move beyond static firmographic targeting toward real-time engagement signals showing who is actively researching and consuming content right now. The platform pairs with a new ICP+ framework that blends declarative and behavioral data for dynamic buying group identification. For D&B, this is a competitive signal worth watching: first-party behavioral data platforms are encroaching on the 'who is in-market' use case that D&B's Buydex and intent-layer offerings also serve, underscoring the need to clearly differentiate on verified entity identity and financial-grade data quality rather than engagement signal volume alone.

stories.headline: Meta Launches 'Meta Compute' Cloud Business, Triggering $200B Chip Stock Selloff
stories.summary: Meta announced plans to commercialize its surplus AI computing capacity under a new unit called 'Meta Compute,' entering the cloud market in direct competition with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. The announcement erased roughly $200 billion in market value from chip and cloud stocks in a single session — Micron fell 10.6%, AMD dropped 6.9% — because it signalled that the AI infrastructure scarcity premium that has driven semiconductor valuations may be cracking. For D&B, this is a pivotal infrastructure economics story: as raw compute shifts from scarce to commoditised, the competitive moat moves up the stack toward proprietary data and entity intelligence — exactly where D&B sits.

stories.headline: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 'Sol' Rolls Out Under New US Frontier Model Safety Review Process — But Scrutiny Process Remains Opaque
stories.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 Sol — a high-end reasoning and coding model — following a new voluntary government pre-release safety review framework established by a June executive order, which gives federal agencies 30 days to vet frontier models before broad release. A TechCrunch investigation found that neither independent experts nor the public have clear visibility into how that review is conducted, with no agreement yet on which agency leads evaluations or what thresholds trigger review. The story matters to D&B because explainability and auditability of AI outputs are becoming regulatory requirements, reinforcing the 'Ground Truth' positioning of D&B data as the verifiable, trusted layer inside agentic workflows.

stories.headline: Together AI Raises $800M Series C at $8.3B Valuation, Led by Aramco Ventures
stories.summary: Together AI, which provides infrastructure for companies running open-source AI models, closed an $800 million Series C at an $8.3 billion valuation with Saudi Aramco's venture arm leading the round. The deal reflects strong enterprise demand for GPU-cloud and model-serving platforms that are model-agnostic — a structural parallel to D&B's own 'be everywhere' neutrality strategy. Sovereign capital from the Gulf anchoring the round also underscores the geopolitical dimension of AI infrastructure ownership that is reshaping the competitive landscape.

stories.headline: EU AI Omnibus Gets Final Council Sign-Off; US Federal AI Bill Stalls on State Preemption Fight
stories.summary: The EU Council gave its final green light on June 29 to the AI Omnibus regulation, pushing the high-risk AI system deadline back to December 2027 for standalone systems and August 2028 for embedded products, while adding new bans on AI-generated sexual deepfakes. Simultaneously, the 'Great American Artificial Intelligence Act' discussion draft collapsed amid bipartisan opposition to federal preemption of state rules — leaving US enterprises facing a patchwork of 109 state-level AI laws with no harmonisation pathway in sight. The divergence between a consolidating EU framework and a fragmenting US one creates compliance complexity that reinforces the value of auditable, explainable AI systems — a direct tailwind for D&B's Ground Truth positioning.

stories.headline: Parliament Backs Universal Credit Boost for 66-Year-Olds as State Pension Age Rises
stories.summary: The cross-party Work and Pensions Committee has backed calls for the Government to increase Universal Credit for 66-year-olds to prevent hardship as the State Pension age rises to 67. The report highlights a gap in support for those caught between the old and new pension ages. Pressure is now on Starmer's government to act ahead of the threshold change.

stories.headline: US Struggles to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as Post-Iran Ceasefire Tensions Persist
stories.summary: With Trump's declared end to the Iran ceasefire, Washington is grappling with the strategic and logistical difficulty of fully reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping. The Washington Post reports the challenge is more complex than the administration's public posture suggests, with naval and diplomatic obstacles remaining. The situation continues to roil global energy markets.

stories.headline: Future Moon Landings Could Destroy Clues to the Origins of Life on Earth
stories.summary: A new study published this week warns that spacecraft exhaust from future lunar missions could rapidly contaminate the Moon's most scientifically valuable regions, potentially masking ancient organic material that holds clues to how life began on Earth. Researchers are calling for new protocols before the next wave of crewed and commercial lunar landings. The findings add urgency to debates about lunar conservation ahead of NASA's Artemis missions.

stories.headline: Tom Kim Wins Genesis Scottish Open at -17 to Head Into The Open in Red-Hot Form
stories.summary: Tom Kim claimed the 2026 Genesis Scottish Open title at the Renaissance Club, finishing at -17 with a final-round 66, two clear of Min Woo Lee in second place. The result gives Kim significant momentum heading into next week's 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale, which tees off on 16 July. The Scottish Open served as the traditional final warm-up for the year's final major.

stories.headline: World Cup Semi-Finals Set: France vs Spain and England vs Argentina — Dream Last-Four Line-Up
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup semi-finals are now confirmed, with France facing Spain in Dallas (Tuesday 14 July) and England taking on defending champions Argentina in Atlanta (Wednesday 15 July) — both matches yet to be played. England reached the last four with a 2-1 win over Norway, Jude Bellingham scoring twice to take his tournament tally to six goals. Argentina edged Switzerland 3-1 after extra time in Kansas City, with Julian Alvarez the hero.

stories.headline: Nations Championship Round 2: England Thrash Fiji 73-8; Injury Blows Mount Ahead of Round 3
stories.summary: England produced a dominant 73-8 demolition of 14-man Fiji in Liverpool to climb to fifth in the World Rugby rankings, while France came from behind to beat Australia 42-26 in Brisbane and Argentina beat Wales 35-21. Injury news is now shaping Round 3 on 18 July: scrum-half Alex Mitchell has been ruled out of England's Argentina trip with a hamstring injury, with Raffi Quirke called up as cover. Scotland's Alex Samuel also faces knee surgery and will miss Saturday's Fiji Test at Murrayfield.

stories.headline: China's Long March-10B Completes Maiden Test Flight With Booster Retrieval — SpaceX Rival Takes Shape
stories.summary: On 10 July, China achieved a significant milestone as its Long March-10B rocket completed its maiden test flight, including the successful retrieval of its first-stage booster — directly mirroring SpaceX's Falcon 9 reusability model. The rocket is designed to support China's crewed lunar programme and is seen as Beijing's most credible challenger to Starship-era launch economics. The test flight signals China is closing the gap on reusable heavy-lift capability faster than many Western analysts expected.

stories.headline: AI Reshapes Software Dealmaking as the Market Splinters Into Tiers of Disruption
stories.summary: ION Analytics / Mergermarket's mid-year analysis finds that global software M&A hit $623bn through June 2026 — up roughly 65% year-on-year — but deal conviction is fracturing sharply along AI exposure lines. Private equity is entering what one M&A lawyer called 'no man's land': processes are dragging, committees are walking away at exclusivity, and valuations are compressing hardest for lower-tier software assets. The key fault line is terminal value, not current earnings — most targets are still hitting their numbers, but buyers can't underwrite what autonomous coding agents like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex do to those businesses in five years. For D&B, this is a direct validation of the moat thesis: Mergermarket notes that 'critical data or security infrastructure providers' are among the assets holding valuation best, exactly the category D&B occupies. The structural question being asked in every deal room right now — 'what proprietary data or workflow control does this asset have that an agent cannot replicate?' — is the question D&B answers with DUNS, Paydex, and 600M+ business records.

stories.headline: PwC Global M&A Mid-Year 2026: AI Is Intensifying the K-Shape — Driving Megadeals While Compressing Value for Exposed Assets
stories.summary: PwC's Global M&A Industry Trends mid-year outlook, published this week, frames 2026 as the year M&A 'supersized' under AI pressure. The report documents a bifurcating market: assets enabling the AI economy — compute, energy, data infrastructure — are attracting premium multiples, while traditional SaaS and services assets face buyer hesitation as AI-related uncertainty grows. Notably, the share of the top 100 corporate deals citing AI as strategic rationale fell from one-third in 2025 to 17% in H1 2026, signalling that buyers are becoming more disciplined about where AI creates durable versus destructive value. PwC also flags that Anthropic, Blackstone, and Goldman Sachs launched a joint enterprise AI services company in May 2026, pointing to a structural shift where AI deployment is becoming a services and integration play — exactly the embedded, neutral-layer model D&B is pursuing. The report reinforces that data infrastructure and workflow-embedded assets are the safest harbour in a repricing storm.
stories.source: PwC Global Deals, Brian Levy (Global Deals Industries Leader)

stories.headline: Enterprise AI Adoption: Data From 161 Engagements Shows the Gap Between Pilots and Production Is Still the Central Problem
stories.summary: Kanerika's enterprise AI adoption report, drawn from 161 real client engagements, confirms that the pilot-to-production gap remains the defining failure mode for enterprise AI in 2026 — echoing patterns from Writer, Deloitte, and Larridin surveys published earlier this year. The data shows that organisations succeeding at scale share three traits: a strong data foundation, clear workflow ownership, and governance frameworks built before deployment rather than retrofitted after. This directly reinforces D&B's 'Entity Resolution as Foundation' pillar — without clean, trusted business entity data, agentic AI workflows break down at the handoff between systems, precisely where most enterprise pilots stall. For D&B's sales motion, this report provides concrete practitioner-level evidence that the bottleneck is data quality and integration readiness, not model capability — making the case for D&B as the ground-truth layer that makes agents deployable.
stories.source: Kanerika (Enterprise AI Adoption Report, based on 161 enterprise engagements)

data_science.tagline: How AI learns to behave well — by asking humans what 'good' looks like
data_science.what_it_is: RLHF is the training technique that bridges raw language model capability and genuine usefulness. A base model (pre-trained on text) is fine-tuned not by labelled examples alone, but by human preference signals — raters compare two model outputs and pick the better one. Those preferences train a separate 'reward model', which then acts as an automated judge. The original LLM is updated via reinforcement learning to maximise that reward. This is how GPT-4, Claude, and Gemini are shaped to follow instructions, avoid harmful outputs, and stay on-topic. For D&B, it is the mechanism that ultimately determines whether an AI agent embedded in Salesforce Agentforce or Microsoft Copilot actually behaves the way a sales rep or risk analyst wants — not just whether it produces fluent text.
data_science.how_it_works: Three stages run in sequence. Stage 1 — Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT): human demonstrators write ideal responses to a set of prompts; the base model is fine-tuned on these to produce a well-behaved starting point. Stage 2 — Reward Model Training: a separate model is trained on human preference pairs (rater picks response A over response B for the same prompt); it learns to score any response on a continuous scale representing human preference. Stage 3 — RL Optimisation (typically PPO — Proximal Policy Optimisation): the SFT model generates responses, the reward model scores them, and PPO updates the policy to increase expected reward. A KL-divergence penalty is applied to stop the policy drifting too far from the SFT baseline (preventing 'reward hacking' — gaming the score without being genuinely better). A lighter-weight modern variant, Direct Preference Optimisation (DPO), skips the explicit reward model entirely and trains directly on preference pairs, making it cheaper and more stable — increasingly preferred for fine-tuning domain-specific models.
data_science.when_to_use: Use RLHF (or DPO as its practical successor) when: (1) the quality of model output is subjective or context-dependent and cannot be captured by a simple accuracy metric — e.g. 'is this sales email the right tone?'; (2) you are deploying a model that interacts directly with end users and alignment to professional norms matters; (3) you need a model to reliably refuse or redirect on out-of-scope queries (critical for agentic workflows where hallucinated company data is a serious risk); (4) you have domain experts who can generate preference labels even if they cannot write training data from scratch. Avoid it when you have fewer than a few thousand preference pairs — DPO becomes unstable, and standard SFT will outperform it. Also avoid when outputs are fully verifiable by a ground-truth metric (classification accuracy, SQL execution success) — use that metric directly as a reward signal instead.
data_science.worked_example: D&B is building a Prospecting Agent that drafts outreach emails using DUNS-resolved firmographic data, Paydex scores, and Buydex intent signals. After initial SFT on example emails written by top-performing SDRs, the agent produces fluent emails — but SDR managers flag two problems: (a) it sometimes cites a company's Paydex score too bluntly ('Your Paydex is 62, indicating payment risk'), which kills deals; (b) it occasionally conflates subsidiary entities with parent companies, producing factually wrong personalisation. A preference labelling exercise is run: 200 SDR managers rate pairs of agent-generated emails on two axes — factual accuracy of entity references and appropriate framing of risk signals. A reward model is trained on ~2,000 preference pairs. DPO fine-tuning is then applied to the Prospecting Agent. Post-training evals show a 34% reduction in entity conflation errors (directly rewarded via the accuracy axis) and SDR open-rate lift of 11% in A/B testing (reflecting the tone axis). The reward model now also acts as a real-time quality gate in production — any draft scoring below threshold is flagged for human review before sending. This is the 'Explainability First' principle operationalised: the reward model's score is an auditable, human-grounded signal, not a black-box confidence score.
resources: Anthropic's 'Constitutional AI' paper — extends RLHF with AI-generated feedback, reducing human labelling burden; directly relevant to scaling domain-specific alignment

Hugging Face TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) library — open-source implementation of PPO, DPO, and reward model training; the fastest practical on-ramp for fine-tuning LLMs with preference data

Rafailov et al. (2023) 'Direct Preference Optimisation: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model' — the foundational DPO paper; essential reading for understanding why most practitioners now prefer DPO over classic PPO-based RLHF
